search_manifest.seed_order: 1-byte seeds first, then 2-byte seeds, each bucket big-endian
search_manifest.match_rule: generated seed prefixes are compared directly against reversible channel bytes
search_manifest.metadata_policy: sidecars, bitmasks, gap streams, and channel descriptors are charged before promotion
search_manifest.scope: grammar/channel research artifact only; not .tlmr format support

channels.name: syntax-skeleton
channels.description: Preserve syntax punctuation while generalizing identifiers, digits, and whitespace.

channels.name: identifier-shape
channels.description: Generalize ASCII identifiers and underscores, preserving other bytes.

channels.name: number-shape
channels.description: Generalize decimal digits, preserving other bytes.

channels.name: whitespace-shape
channels.description: Normalize whitespace classes, preserving non-whitespace bytes.

channels.name: field-delimiter-skeleton
channels.description: Preserve line and field delimiters while generalizing payload bytes.

channels.name: all-class-stream
channels.description: Emit one byte class per input byte and charge the whole literal sidecar.

channels.name: class-run-stream
channels.description: Emit class/run-length pairs and charge the whole literal sidecar.

channels.name: punctuation-gap-stream
channels.description: Emit only structural punctuation and charge non-structural bytes plus gap lengths.

summary.conclusion: Grammar channels did not produce prefix>=5, exact, selected, or metadata-profitable rows.
